title: Patch the Vulnerable Logging Library
objective: Upgrade the vulnerable logging library to a version where the JNDI remote-code-execution flaw is fixed.
briefing: Logs show a JNDI lookup was triggered from a request header - the outdated logging library (2.14.1) is vulnerable and needs to be upgraded, not just reconfigured.
hints: Restarting the web server keeps loading the same vulnerable jar.
The fix is upgrading the dependency itself to a version where this is patched.
debrief: Upgrading to 2.17.0 or later removes the vulnerable JNDI lookup behavior from message logging entirely (these versions disable message-lookup substitution by default) - anything below that line is still exploitable by a related JNDI-lookup variant even if the very first Log4Shell payload stops working. For Vulnerable and Outdated Components (A06:2021), patch-level reconfiguration isn't the durable fix here - the dependency itself needs upgrading, and the real long-term fix is an ongoing process: maintain a software bill of materials (SBOM), track CVEs against your dependency versions, and have a fast path to patch them, rather than discovering the version only after an incident.

logs: [VULN] log4j 2.14.1 evaluated JNDI lookup from User-Agent header - RCE triggered

cases.output:
removing log4j-core-2.14.1.jar
installing log4j-core-{version}.jar
JNDI message lookups disabled by default... done

logs_append: [FIX] log4j upgraded to {version} - JNDI lookups disabled by default

cases.output:
removing log4j-core-2.14.1.jar
installing log4j-core-{version}.jar
WARNING: {version} is still vulnerable to a JNDI-lookup RCE variant below 2.17.0... done

logs_append: [VULN] upgraded to {version}, still below the safe threshold (2.17.0)
